original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?

This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in WebUI Bug 279019?
original_answer: WebUI (Bug ID 279019): Symptom: After uploading an invalid PEM certificate, the hpe-restd process becomes unstable, the WebUI temporarily stops responding, and all REST API calls from the WebUI fail. Scenario: If a user uploads a corrupt PEM certificate file using WebUI certificate management window, selecting the Upload button in the WebUI causes the WebUI to stop working completely. To recover from this state, restart hpe-restd from the bash prompt in the command-line interface or restart the switch. Workaround: Use the CLI to upload certificates.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in Internal srvcs: Security PA infra Bug 278954?
original_answer: Internal srvcs: Security PA infra (Bug ID 278954): Symptom: Clients are onboarding with an incorrect auth-priority order. Users onboarded with a lower-priority authentication method, so higher-priority authentication requests were not seen. Scenario: When concurrent onboarding is enabled with a default authentication priority and non-default auth-precedence on a port, clients onboarding are taking the wrong auth-priority. Workaround: At the issue state, reconfigure the auth-priority on the port to onboard the clients.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what issue was resolved in TPM Bug 265125?
original_answer: TPM (Bug ID 265125): Symptom: Failure to establish or maintain a connection to Aruba Central. Scenario: A switch may fail to establish or maintain its relationship with Aruba Central. In these cases, the switch may display the "Central source connection status" field in the "show aruba-central" command will read "connection_failure". Workaround: Issue the following commands in order: start-shell; sudo bash; systemctl restart_jitterentropy-rngd; exit; exit; show aruba-central.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. For new certificate names, only alphanumeric characters, dots, dashes, and underscores are allowed.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.0010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1000, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1005, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 289568): Symptom: The root switch initiates STP re-convergence and causes causing MAC flush and traffic loss. Scenario: This issue occurs during an upgrade process in VSX pair (which acts as non-root switch in RPVST topology). When the primary switch is down, this issue is encountered in a non-root access switch connected to this VSX-pair. Workaround: Install BPDU filter on the root-port of access switch during the software upgrade and remove the filter after the upgrade.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what issue was resolved in Central Bug 293314?
original_answer: Central (Bug ID 293314): Symptom: The client list is not populated in the Central client view page. Scenario: In very rare instances, after maintenance is performed on Aruba Central infrastructure, the client list in the Central client view page may fail to list the connected clients for some switches. This issue is uncommon enough that an upgrade to this release may not be warranted if the only reason for the upgrade is to avoid this potential issue. Workaround: From the switch console, enter bash via the start-shell command, and execute the command systemctl restart yang-resolverd.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?

This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Hot Patch?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Hot Patch - When a hot-patch file download is triggered using the switch WebUI, log messages can incorrectly state that the file is added to the database with a missing status. This is a temporary state, and will correctly change to Not applied once the download is completed.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. If a local or external SNMP MIB walk is performed in the interval between when SNMP is first enabled and the SNMP daemon is ready, the MIB walk action will return an error.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 306746?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 306746): Symptom: The last_configuration_time is not updated on the switch. Workaround: If the NTP configuration must be updated via CLI, then toggle the management interface from On to Off and then toggle it back to On.

reason: The answer does not directly address the issue resolved in Bug 305843. It provides a workaround instead of describing the bug and its resolution.
original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1020, what issue was resolved in ACL Logging Bug 305843?
original_answer: ACL Logging (Bug ID 305843): Symptom: Switch reloads unexpectedly during a firmware upgrade. Scenario: This issue occurs during an upgrade where a switch might reload while parsing an Access List (ACL) configuration. Workaround: It is recommended to follow the following procedure before the upgrade: Save the full configuration as a checkpoint using the copy running-config checkpoint <name> command. Remove all access-list configurations, save the configuration as the startup configuration using the copy running-config startup-config command. Upgrade the switch. Once the switch is upgraded, move the ACLs back to the running configuration by copying the saved checkpoint to the running configuration using the copy checkpoint <name> running-config command.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_answer: Central (Bug ID 300693): Symptom: Firmware updates via Aruba Central fails with an event log, Event | 4403 | LOG_ERR | CDTR | 1 | User admin: primary image update failed via HTTPS from common.cloud.hpe.com. Scenario: This issue is observed when the switch is configured to use http-proxy and the switch's upstream DNS server does not have an entry for common.cloud.hpe.com and when DNS recursion is disabled. Workaround: Configure a static DNS entry for common.cloud.hpe.com using the ip dns host command.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1031, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1040, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?

If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what caveat is documented for Certificates?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Certificates - When a switch uses a certificate with a legacy certificate name that is not supported in 10.12 because it contains disallowed characters, the information will migrate properly in the upgrade, but that certificate can no longer be edited. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1050, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1060, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1070, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 338204?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1080, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what caveat is documented for Central?

If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1090, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 335246?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 335246): Symptom: Delay of ~30s observed when updating VLAN access config via snmpset and verifying via snmpget immediately after. Workaround: Use the CLI for VLAN access configuration instead of SNMP.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1101, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 360748?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 360748): Symptom: BGP selects the wrong path, causing routing issues. Scenario: In some scenarios BGP incorrectly selects the path with the highest AS-Path. This causes incorrect paths to be propagated to the Routing Information Base (RIB) and Forwarding Information Base (FIB), potentially causing routing issues. Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. The higher local preference route is selected.

Workaround: Lower the local preference of EVPN originated router. The higher local preference route is selected.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 367181?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 367181): Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with snmp-server response-source prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file /fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf, or 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing [snmp] srcipaddr entry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what is the workaround for SNMP Bug 367181?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 367181): The documented workaround is: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file /fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf, or 2. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1110, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what caveat is documented for Central?

This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1120, what issue was resolved in Logging Bug 358847?
original_answer: Logging (Bug ID 358847): Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal:6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 373232?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 373232): Symptom: Multicast traffic loss may occur on interfaces with PIM-Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) enabled in overlay networks. Affected multicast flows fail to replicate across VXLAN tunnels, resulting in service disruption. Scenario: In VSX deployments, multicast replication may fail during system boot or reload if the associated L3 interfaces for overlay VLANs are not fully initialized. This is more likely in configurations involving MCLAGs and delayed VSX synchronization, where multicast processing begins before the L3 interface is ready. Workaround: Toggle the pim-sparse configuration on the impacted interfaces.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1130, what issue was resolved in EVPN Bug 364576?
original_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 364576): Symptom: Missing ARP entries showing as "incomplete" in the ARP table, causing connectivity issues in remote VTEP. Scenario: This issue may occur in an EVPN VXLAN environment with VSX pairs when a VIP (Virtual IP) moves from one node to another node, MAC address aging occurs or after executing "clear mac-address" on the switch. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 371717?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what issue was resolved in Classifier Bug 335358?
original_answer: Classifier (Bug ID 335358): Symptom: IP packets containing options headers bypass routed-in security policies configured with drop actions, resulting in policy enforcement failure. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1140, what issue was resolved in PVST Bug 389395?
original_answer: PVST (Bug ID 389395): Symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state. Scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active. Workaround: Shut down all affected ports. Restart the hpe-pvstd process using the following commands: start-shell sudo bash ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep systemctl restart hpe-pvstd ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep exit exit

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what caveat is documented for Central?

This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1150, what issue was resolved in Container Manager Bug 366132?
original_answer: Container Manager (Bug ID 366132): Symptom: Container statuses are not updated after deleting and recreating the containers. Scenario: This issue can occur with these following time sensitive steps: 1. Container A is created, Container Manager is processing the creation of said container. 2. A delete request for container A is sent using CLI or REST. 3. A request to create container A is sent. 4. When checking for statuses using REST or show container, the container is marked as down. Workaround: Where possible, avoid deleting and recreating the same container while it is being created, allow it to reach the operational state. If the issue is encountered, delete the container using REST or CLI and restart the switch to release the occupied container resources.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what caveat is documented for Central?

This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 277126?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 277126): Symptom: Classifier or PBR Policy is not enforced for traffic coming via ISL link. Scenario: In a VSX+ VRRP topology, if a policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting the classifier policy enforced. Workaround: To prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL, use active gateway instead of VRRP. This provides better active-active redundancy and packets reaching either VSX switch process the packet destined to active gateway.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1160, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 277126?
cleaned_answer: PBR (Bug ID 277126): Symptom: Classifier or PBR Policy not enforced for traffic via ISL link. Scenario: In a VSX+ VRRP topology, if a policy is applied on SVI, all packets reaching the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL and are not getting the classifier policy enforced. Workaround: Use active gateway instead of VRRP for better redundancy; packets reaching either VSX switch process the packet destined to the active gateway.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. If the Aruba Central feature is enabled using this command, the switch will then reconnect back to Aruba Central and will get disconnected again. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 400513?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 400513): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.13.1170, what issue was resolved in PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.0001, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 215677?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 215677): Symptom: IGMP/MLD configuration changes do not take effect when the hpe-mgmdd process is flooded with IGMP/MLD control packets. Scenario: In a situation where IGMP or MLD control packets (Joins or Leaves) are part of a loop, or if misbehaving clients send continuous streams of IGMP/MLD control packets, the hpe-mgmdd daemon's packet queue can become continuously full. Workaround: Restart the MGMD daemon using the command systemctl restart hpe-mgmdd.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.0006, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.0007, what caveat is documented for Central?
cleaned_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will be disconnected. This cycle of connecting and disconnecting continues until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To prevent this unnecessary reconnection, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until registration or licensing occurs.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1000, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1020, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 338204?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status need to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what issue was resolved in IGMP Bug 338204?
cleaned_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 338204): Symptom: An IPTV may experience a delay when changing channels. This problem may occur intermittently depending on the number of multicast entries. Scenario: The multicast entries present in the database need to be updated with the activity-status every 30 seconds. The activity-status needs to be fetched from the hardware for all the multicast entries. This is taking 5-6 seconds of time in the customer network. So, the channel change, which is leading to the forwarding port change in the hardware, is pending behind the update of activity-status of multicast entries.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1040, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1050, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 335246?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 335246): Symptom: Delay of ~30s observed when updating VLAN access config via snmpset and verifying via snmpget immediately after. Workaround: Use the CLI for VLAN access configuration instead of SNMP.

reason: The original question and answer are unclear due to the use of '9300S_10040' which is not relevant to the known issue. The cleaned version removes this irrelevant information.
original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1060, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.14.1061, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch is able to connect to Aruba Central but is not registered in the Aruba Central inventory or does not have a proper license, the switch will get disconnected. This connect/disconnect process will continue until the switch is properly registered in Aruba Central. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, best practice is to disable Aruba Central until the switch is registered in Aruba Central, or a license is obtained for that device.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what issue was resolved in Spanning Tree Bug 329044?
original_answer: Spanning Tree (Bug ID 329044): Symptom: An RPVST daemon daemon restart occurred. Scenario: This issue can occur when connecting an STP-aware switch to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured. Ensure that the peer switch has an RPVST VLAN that is not configured on the switch. Workaround: Refrain from connecting STP-aware switches to these ports. Alternatively, temporarily remove the BPDU-guard configuration to prevent further daemon restarts.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.0001, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 317831?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 317831): Symptom: When configuring a VLAN outside of the VLAN range through REST, the hpe-entityd process daemon restarts. Scenario: When a VLAN configured through REST is outside the range of allowable VLAN values, hpe-entityd daemon restarts when attempting to match ports to currently configured VLANs. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what issue was resolved in Spanning Tree Bug 329044?
original_answer: Spanning Tree (Bug ID 329044): Symptom: An RPVST daemon daemon restart occurred. Scenario: This issue can occur when connecting an STP-aware switch to a port where spanning-tree guard is configured. Ensure that the peer switch has an RPVST VLAN that is not configured on the switch. Workaround: Refrain from connecting STP-aware switches to these ports. Alternatively, temporarily remove the BPDU-guard configuration to prevent further daemon restarts.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.0005, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1005, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in MSTP Bug 346938?
original_answer: MSTP (Bug ID 346938): Symptom: Intermittent MSTP/RPVST starvation messages observed coming from source mac address 00:00:00:00:00:00. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in Mirroring Bug 342437?
original_answer: Mirroring (Bug ID 342437): Symptom: Packets near MTU limits in size may not be present in a port or tunnel/remote based mirror. Scenario: When a client configures a mirror where the destination interface has the same MTU size, mirrored packets that are close to max MTU are incorrectly dropped and not mirrored. This applies to remote/tunnel mirrors as well. Workaround: Configuring mirror destination ports as routing port and set the IP MTU size. For a VLAN interface, set the IP MTU on the VLAN SVI. IP MTU must be set to accommodate the ethernet overhead +tag. For the default MTU, 1500, the IP MTU would be 1518. For a tunneled scenario, the MTU must also account for the tunnel header.

Scenario: Configure port-access on a port (MAC_Auth/Dot1x/port-security). Setup the client MAC that is trying to onboard such that the MAC is denied. For example: Either by failing authentication or due to max client limit. Move such a client to a non-secure port. Observe the MAC table that the MAC is not moved to the non-secure port.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in L3 Routes Bug 340461?
original_answer: L3 Routes (Bug ID 340461): Symptom: Active IPv4 route is not displayed even though SVI is in up state. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 339717?
original_answer: VXLAN (Bug ID 339717): Symptom: ops-switchd crash while collecting VXLAN diagdump or collecting support files all. Scenario: In some VXLAN scenarios with multiple LAGs and multiple overlay VLAN on those lags. After device reboot, the LAG interface could show incorrect VXLAN Programming. If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGS, a ops-switchd crash might be observed. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring the VLANs for the lag.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in VXLAN Bug 339717?

If the user attempts to remove VLANs, VNI-to-VXLAN configurations that belong to the affected LAGs might cause a ops-switchd crash. Workaround: Ensure LAG interfaces are up before configuring the VLANs for the lag.

original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 335246): Symptom: Delay of ~30s observed when updating VLAN access config via snmpset and verifying via snmpget immediately after. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what issue was resolved in ACL Bug 234200?
original_answer: ACL (Bug ID 234200): Symptom: ACL fails to apply only the VSX secondary switch. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1010, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_answer: DHCP Server (Bug ID 408254): Symptom: DHCP-Server configuration status becomes stuck in pending state after configuration changes are pushed from Aruba Central, preventing proper DHCP service operation. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 367181?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 367181): Symptom: Switches may fail to respond to SNMP queries after upgrading from 10.08 version of code to 10.13 code. Scenario: This issue occurs when a switch has been configured with snmp-server response-source prior to upgrade. Workaround: After upgrading, access the switch shell and either: 1. Delete the file /fs/nos/net-snmp/agent-swns/snmp.conf, or 2. Edit the file to comment out (with #) the line containing [snmp] srcipaddr entry 3. Restart the SNMP service with systemctl restart snmpd-wrapper-in-c.service.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in RADIUS Bug 363074?
original_answer: RADIUS (Bug ID 363074): Symptom: The switch intermittently fails to include the Framed-IP attribute in RADIUS accounting packets. This results in missing IP address information for authenticated clients. Scenario: The issue is observed when a client disconnects and reconnects after a short interval (for example, 90 seconds). The Framed-IP attribute may be missing even though the client has a valid IP address and is visible in other system outputs. Workaround: Ensure continuous client traffic after reconnection to allow the switch to relearn the IP address. Alternatively, re-enable MAC authentication on the port or perform a shut/no shut cycle to restore expected behavior.
cleaned_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what issue was resolved in RADIUS Bug 363074?
cleaned_answer: RADIUS (Bug ID 363074): Symptom: The switch intermittently fails to include the Framed-IP attribute in RADIUS accounting packets, resulting in missing IP address information for authenticated clients. Scenario: This issue is observed when a client disconnects and reconnects after a short interval (e.g., 90 seconds). The Framed-IP attribute may be missing even though the client has a valid IP address and is visible in other system outputs. Workaround: Ensure continuous client traffic after reconnection to allow the switch to relearn the IP address. Alternatively, re-enable MAC authentication on the port or perform a shut/no shut cycle to restore expected behavior.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1030, what known issue is documented for IGMP Bug 341085?
original_answer: IGMP (Bug ID 341085): Symptom: Multicast traffic is being blocked after upgrading from AOS-CX 10.12.0006 or earlier versions of AOS-CX. Scenario: If the switch has a control plane ACL configured AND has IGMP or PIM configured on a pre-10.12.1000 build and upgrades to 10.12.1000 or a later release, the implicit deny in the control plane ACL will block multicast traffic thus inhibiting functionality of those features. Workaround: Manually add an ACE to the control plane ACL to explicitly permit multicast traffic.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, what issue was resolved in Health Monitor Bug 365600?
original_answer: Health Monitor (Bug ID 365600): Symptom: Diagnostic failure messages observed in the switch event log for multiple components (pmc, fepld2, fepld1, mcb, icb) on the management module with error code 0x2. Scenario: This issue may occur during regular switch operation due an internal memory mapping call. Workaround: Restarting the Hardware Health Monitor Daemon (HHMD) using the systemctl restart hhmd command temporarily resolves the issue. This allows diagnostics to pass correctly until the issue reoccurs.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1040, what issue was resolved in L3 Routes Bug 365274?
original_answer: L3 Routes (Bug ID 365274): Symptom: The Network Management Systems does not display IPv4 addresses for Aruba CX switches running AOS-CX version 10.13. Scenario: This issue occurs when the NMS queries the switch using SNMP and relies on either of the following OIDs to retrieve IPv4 address information: 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.20.1.2 (deprecated) 1.3.6.1.2.1.4.34.1.5 (current but not accessible in 10.13) In AOS-CX 10.13, neither OID returns valid IPv4 data, resulting in incomplete device visibility.

original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 373232): Symptom: Multicast traffic loss may occur on interfaces with PIM-Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) enabled in overlay networks. Affected multicast flows fail to replicate across VXLAN tunnels, resulting in service disruption. Scenario: In VSX deployments, multicast replication may fail during system boot or reload if the associated L3 interfaces for overlay VLANs are not fully initialized. This is more likely in configurations involving MCLAGs and delayed VSX synchronization, where multicast processing begins before the L3 interface is ready. Workaround: Toggle the pim-sparse configuration on the impacted interfaces.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1050, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 371717?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.15.1060, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in Switch Profiles Bug 351884?
original_answer: Switch Profiles (Bug ID 351884): Symptom: Starting in 10.16.1006, the default system profile for the 9300/9300S platforms is "DC-Optimized-Leaf". DC-Optimized profiles do not support IP tunnels (GRE, 6in4, 6in6). Scenario: Starting in 10.16.1006, the default system profile for the 9300/9300S platforms is "DC-Optimized-Leaf". DC-Optimized profiles do not support IP tunnels (GRE, 6in4, 6in6). Workaround: If desired the switch profile can be manually changed back to the previous default system profile of "Leaf". original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in Logging Bug 358847?
original_answer: Logging (Bug ID 358847): Symptom: A VSF stack member switch unexpectedly reboots due to the ops-switchd process crashing with a signal: 6 error. Scenario: In some scenarios with excessive ACL event logging, a crash may occur due to a failure in the system when processing a high volume of ACL log entries. Workaround: Minimize the use of the log keyword in ACL entries, especially on high-traffic rules. Consider using ACL counters instead of logging for monitoring traffic patterns.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in L3 routes Bug 365274?

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in DHCP Relay Bug 371954?
original_answer: DHCP Relay (Bug ID 371954): Symptom: In an L2VPN network, when a DHCP client sends an IP request with the broadcast flag unset (unicast), duplicate DHCP client packets are observed at the DHCP server-one from each VTEP. As a result, the client does not receive an IP address. Scenario: To prevent duplicate DHCP client packets from each VTEP in an L2VPN topology, it is recommended to configure "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" on the switch. However, after saving the switch configuration and rebooting, the relay daemon does not correctly apply all configuration settings at boot time, even though the configuration persists in the database. This causes the duplicate packet issue to reappear. Workaround: To restore correct behavior, manually enable and then disable the L2VPN client configuration using "dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients" followed by "no dhcp-relay l2vpn-clients".

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1006, what issue was resolved in VSX Bug 373232?
original_answer: VSX (Bug ID 373232): Symptom: Multicast traffic loss may occur on interfaces with PIM-Sparse Mode (PIM-SM) enabled in overlay networks. Affected multicast flows fail to replicate across VXLAN tunnels, resulting in service disruption. Scenario: In VSX deployments, multicast replication may fail during system boot or reload if the associated L3 interfaces for overlay VLANs are not fully initialized. This is more likely in configurations involving MCLAGs and delayed VSX synchronization, where multicast processing begins before the L3 interface is ready. Workaround: Toggle the pim-sparse configuration on the impacted interfaces.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what caveat is documented for SNMP?

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what issue was resolved in NTP Bug 371717?
original_answer: NTP (Bug ID 371717): Symptom: Excessive connection lost and connection established logging when multiple NTP servers are configured. Scenario: If the NTP server changes, a connection lost and connection established is logged to the event log. Workaround: The fix disables logging of normal server changes and only logs events if server reachability reaches 0 or NTP synchronization is lost.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what issue was resolved in Classifier Bug 335358?
original_answer: Classifier (Bug ID 335358): Symptom: IP packets containing options headers bypass routed-in security policies configured with drop actions, resulting in policy enforcement failure. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what issue was resolved in PVST Bug 389395?
original_answer: PVST (Bug ID 389395): Symptom: An STP port incorrectly transitions into Disabled/Blocked state. Scenario: During certain port flap events, STP may erroneously mark a port as Disabled/Blocked, even when the link should remain active. Workaround: Shut down all affected ports. Restart the hpe-pvstd process using the following commands: start-shell sudo bash ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep systemctl restart hpe-pvstd ps aux | grep hpe-pvstd | grep -v grep exit exit

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1010, what issue was resolved in EVPN Bug 372373?
original_answer: EVPN (Bug ID 372373): Symptom: Traffic loss occurs for existing networks in a bundle when the VLAN-aware bundle is disabled or enabled for VLAN-ethernet-tag updates. Scenario: This issue is observed during VXLAN interoperability with Juniper Networks devices, particularly when updating VLAN-ethernet-tag mappings. The problem arises when the entire bundle is disabled, affecting all traffic associated with it. Workaround: Use the same VLAN ID and VNI ID values on both the Juniper and Aruba devices.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. To avoid this unnecessary reconnection cycle, it is best practice to disable HPE Aruba Networking Central until the switch is registered or a license is obtained.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1020, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 400261?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 400261): Symptom: SNMP OID sysUpTimeInstance (1.3.6.1.2.1.1.3.0) may return an incorrect value when polled, leading to inaccurate uptime reporting in monitoring systems. Scenario: This issue occurs on switches running AOS-CX 10.16 when using SNMP to query sysUpTimeInstance. The reported value does not match the actual system uptime, causing discrepancies in monitoring tools. Workaround: Use the OID hrSystemUptime (1.3.6.1.2.1.25.1.1.0) instead of sysUpTimeInstance for accurate system uptime reporting.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what issue was resolved in SNMP Bug 408309?
original_answer: SNMP (Bug ID 408309): Symptom: SNMPv3 user password changes do not take effect, after deleting and re-creating the user with a new password, the device still accepts the old password and rejects the new one. Scenario: This issue may occur when deleting an existing SNMPv3 user and re-adding the same username with a different 'auth-pass'; authentication succeeds only with the original password. Workaround: Create a new SNMPv3 user with the desired password and do not reuse the original username.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1030, what known issue is documented for Classifier Bug 408826?
original_answer: Classifier (Bug ID 408826): Symptom: CPU-transmitted packets are not subjected to actions configured in policies applied to route-only ports on egress. This includes traffic originating directly from the switch, such as pings, as well as traffic forwarded to the CPU as part of the initial learning of a traffic flow. Scenario: This issue occurs when a policy is applied to a route-only port on egress and CPU-transmitted packets egress the port. Workaround: If the policy has any drop actions, use an egress ACL on the port to block the traffic instead.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what caveat is documented for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - When SNMP is enabled via the switch CLI, it can take between 1-2 minutes for the SNMP daemon to be ready to respond to requests. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what limitation is mentioned for SNMP?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: SNMP - Overlay/system partition information is included in HOST-RESOURCES-MIB polling. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what issue was resolved in MAC Tables Bug 415095?
original_answer: MAC Tables (Bug ID 415095): Symptom: MACs are only showing on one side of a VSX pair. For instance, a MAC is learned on a physical port on one VSX device and is missing from the peer VSX device. Scenario: This issue can occur after resolving a network loop which causes the MACs to quickly move from one VSX device to the other, particularly when MACs are moving from EVPN tunnels to physical ports. Workaround: Using the CLI to clear the problematic MAC address.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what known issue is documented for PBR Bug 407418?
original_answer: PBR (Bug ID 407418): Symptom: Classifier Policy is not enforced for traffic coming from an ISL link. Scenario: For a topology with VSX+ VRRP, if the policy is applied on SVI, all packets which reach the VRRP secondary device go to the primary device via the ISL. All of those packets are not getting classifier policy enforced. Even though the policy action is PBR redirect, those packets are not getting redirected. Workaround: Two workarounds are possible to prevent packets from being forwarded to VSX primary via ISL. 1. Use active gateway to give better active active redundancy. Packets reaching either VSX device process the packet destined to active gateway. 2. Turn off the interface connection from VRRP secondary device to down switch. This method may cause redundancy and is not preferred.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.16.1040, what known issue is documented for VLAN Mapping Bug 398061?
original_answer: VLAN Mapping (Bug ID 398061): Symptom: Traffic disruption occurs after globally removing a VLAN associated with both a VLAN Translation rule and VXLAN interface. Scenario: This issue occurs when following these steps: 1. Create a VLAN and associating to VXLAN and a host connected port. 2. On a host connected port, configure VLAN translate 210 to 410. 3. Then delete VLAN 410 globally. Workaround: Reconfigure vlan trunk allow 210 on a host connected port.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect. This cycle will continue until the switch is properly registered or a license is obtained. original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.17.0001, what issue was resolved in BGP Bug 372373?
original_answer: BGP (Bug ID 372373): Symptom: Ethernet-tag mappings are permitted only when the operational state of the EVPN VLAN-aware bundle is disabled. Scenario: In previous versions of AOS-CX, modifications to VLAN: Ethernet-tag mappings were permitted only when the operational state of the EVPN VLAN-aware bundle was disabled. In AOS-CX 10.17, this restriction has been relaxed-modifications are now allowed regardless of the operational state, provided that the resulting effective Ethernet tag does not conflict with any existing VLAN tag assignment. This ensures configuration flexibility while maintaining tag uniqueness and preventing forwarding conflicts.

original_question: For 9300S_10040 AOS-CX 10.17.1001, what caveat is documented for Central?
original_answer: Feature Caveat: Central - When a switch can connect to HPE Aruba Networking Central but is not registered in the inventory or lacks a proper license, it will repeatedly disconnect and reconnect.
